project-app.title: Meeting Optimization App
project-app.summary:
This project was part of a 4-week launch project for graduation at CodeYourFuture. We developed a full-stack app that helps organisers find the best time and location for events by optimising based on journeys across multiple potential locations and times. The app uses the Google Routes API to calculate routes from attendee locations to destinations, analyses the data on the backend, and displays the results to organisers.
The app currently focuses on event optimisation but will soon include attendee input and a mobile app for easier access. Our custom algorithm compares event options, and we plan to enhance the app further by allowing attendees to input their data and enabling organisers to choose the most convenient details for all participants.

project-aut.title: User Authentication Api
project-aut.summary: I developed an API to manage user credentials, authentication, and role-based authorization for use across my other portfolio apps. The API allows users to create an API key, which is used by other applications to create, authenticate, authorize, update, and delete users. By centralizing user management, this API eliminates the need for repetitive functionality in each app. It has been integrated into my other portfolio applications for efficient user management.
keyPoints: User Sign-In and JWT Token Issuance

project-read.title: Autogenerated Portfolio App
project-read.summary: This project aims to automate the creation of a graduate directory by collecting activity data from CodeYourFuture graduates GitHub profiles. The platform showcases their skills, contributions, and projects, keeping the directory up-to-date. It helps potential employers, mentors, and collaborators easily access relevant information, facilitating networking and career opportunities within the tech community. Using GitHub API integrations, the platform automatically updates each graduate's profile and assigns an activity score based on their GitHub usage. The system streamlines the process for graduates to showcase their work while offering employers an efficient way to assess and connect with potential candidates.
keyPoints: GitHub Graphql and Rest Api Usage
Role-Based Access for Graduates, Mentors, and Recruiters

project-mess.title: Secure Message App
project-mess.summary:
I developed a messaging app that combines WebSocket and HTTP requests for secure communication. In the current version, WebSocket is used to trigger real-time updates, while HTTP is used to securely store and retrieve messages.
The upcoming Messaging v2 API will offer greater flexibility by providing a customizable messaging frame for other apps. These apps can adjust the frame’s appearance and functionality, control who can message whom, set message limits, and decide if users can message each other or only a support team. The frame is easy to integrate, and messages are securely stored while real-time updates are triggered through WebSocket.
If you're interested in collaborating or exploring how this could enhance your apps, feel free to reach out!
keyPoints: User Sends Message via HTTP Request
Other Users Requests Updates via HTTP
Users Receive Message via HTTP Request
Message History Is Stored and Managed

project-map.title: Statistical Data Encoded Maps
project-map.summary:
As part of a medical research project at Manchester University, I contributed to the creation of choropleth maps to visualize the population distribution of specific groups of people. I started by processing postcode data from a CSV document, which I stored in a database. Using the Postcodes.io API, I converted postcodes into detailed address objects, extracting information at the country, region, county, and local district levels.
I then aggregated the data at these geographical levels, calculating the population distribution for each area. The results were mapped to visualize population density across various administrative regions. To ensure accurate and up-to-date mapping, I utilized National Statistics maps and QGIS software for data encoding and visualization.
In addition, I am currently working on automating this entire process to improve the efficiency of future research projects, enabling faster data processing and map generation.
